title: How to make a blog comfortable to read on mobile
preview: A closer look at article layout, typography, content blocks, and the UX choices that make mobile reading feel effortless.
author: Maria Kovalenko

content.text: The screen structure should guide the reader
On a mobile screen it is important to show the title, a short intro, and a clear content hierarchy right away. Readers should instantly understand what matters most.
Formatted text and visual breathing room
A mix of subheadings, short paragraphs, and images makes an article feel lighter and easier to scan. Users get tired less quickly and find key ideas faster.

title: Why Expo is a strong fit for a study React Native project
preview: How Expo speeds up setup, simplifies Android testing, and removes a lot of routine configuration work at the start.
author: Ihor Shevchenko

content.text: A quick start without extra configuration
Expo helps us focus on interface and application logic quickly. For a study blog app that means less time on native setup and more time on actual features.
At the beginning it is enough to think through folders, screens, and mock data. After that we can iteratively add comments, search, likes, or backend integration.
